name: Dullahan
description: The Dullahan, also known as the Headless Horseman of Irish folklore, is a terrifying harbinger of death. This spectral figure rides a black horse through the countryside, carrying his own severed head under his arm. The head glows with an eerie light, illuminating the path and allowing the Dullahan to see for vast distances, even on the darkest nights. Where he rides, death follows. It is said that he calls out the name of the person destined to die, and upon hearing it, they immediately perish. The Dullahan is a creature of immense power and dread, a solitary figure forever bound to his grim task. Some tales suggest that he uses a human spine as a whip, further emphasizing his connection to death and decay. He is a formidable figure, feared throughout the Irish countryside, and a chilling reminder of mortality. His appearance alone is enough to strike terror into the hearts of even the bravest souls. The Dullahan is often associated with festivals like Samhain, when the veil between worlds is thin, making it easier for supernatural entities to cross over. He is a powerful symbol of death and the unknown, a figure that continues to haunt the imagination and remind us of the fragility of life.
subtitle: Headless Horseman of Irish lore
appearance: The Dullahan is a headless horseman, typically depicted riding a black horse. He carries his own severed head, which glows with an unearthly light. His skin is often described as pale or decaying, and his eyes are wide and staring. He may use a human spine as a whip. The horse is usually described as being large, black, and with fiery eyes.
habitat: Rural areas, dark country roads, and places associated with death or burial grounds in Ireland.
behavior: The Dullahan rides at night, searching for the person whose death he foresees. He is silent except for the sound of his horse's hooves and the occasional chilling laugh. He is relentless in his pursuit and cannot be stopped by obstacles such as locked gates, which he simply rides through. He is a solitary figure, driven by an unknown purpose.

keyMyths.title: The Call of Death
keyMyths.description: The Dullahan calls out the name of the person destined to die, and upon hearing it, they immediately perish. This highlights his role as a harbinger of death and the power of his voice.

sources.author: W.B. Yeats
sources.work: Fairy and Folk Tales of the Irish Peasantry
sources.description: A collection of Irish folklore, including stories of the Dullahan.
sources.text: A collection of Irish folklore, including stories of the Dullahan.

sources.author: Patricia Lysaght
sources.work: The Banshee: The Irish Supernatural Death Messenger
sources.description: While focused on the Banshee, it provides context on Irish death omens and beliefs related to the supernatural.
sources.text: While focused on the Banshee, it provides context on Irish death omens and beliefs related to the supernatural.
